title: Xtra Flaky American Red Snapper Skillet
description: A buttery and flaky one-pot American dish featuring red snapper, this recipe is perfect for a special occasion dinner. The ice-bath-shock step ensures the fish is cooked to perfection.
instructions: Rinse the red snapper fillets under cold water, then pat them dry with paper towels.
Season the fillets with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and paprika.
Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the red snapper fillets to the skillet, skin side up, and cook for 3 minutes.
Flip the fillets over and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
Remove the fillets from the skillet and immediately submerge them in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Let the fillets sit in the ice bath for 5 minutes, then remove them and pat dry with paper towels.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter to the skillet and stir in the lemon juice.
Return the red snapper fillets to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes, spooning the butter sauce over the top of the fish.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve immediately.
